semiconductor.overview: The semiconductor industry is cyclical with high capital intensity. Current cycle (2024-2025) driven by AI demand, particularly HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) for AI accelerators.
semiconductor.market_dynamics: Key drivers: AI/ML training demand, data center expansion, smartphone recovery, automotive electronics. Inventory correction cycle largely complete as of 2024.
semiconductor.etf_impact: Semiconductor ETFs are HIGHLY sensitive to: earnings reports from major players, AI demand indicators, China export controls, memory pricing trends.

samsung electronics.overview: World's largest memory chip maker (DRAM, NAND) and major smartphone manufacturer. Listed on KOSPI (005930.KS).

business_segments.DX: Device eXperience - smartphones, TVs, appliances (~40% revenue)
business_segments.DS: Device Solutions - memory, foundry, display (~50% revenue)

samsung electronics.key_metrics: Memory market share: DRAM ~40%, NAND ~35%. HBM market share growing rapidly to challenge SK Hynix.
samsung electronics.etf_impact: Samsung-tracking ETFs (7747.HK 2x long, 7347.HK 2x inverse) are DIRECTLY impacted by: Samsung earnings, memory pricing, smartphone market share, HBM orders from NVIDIA.

sk hynix.overview: Second-largest DRAM manufacturer globally. Primary HBM supplier to NVIDIA. Listed on KOSPI (000660.KS).

business_segments.DRAM: ~70% revenue, strong in HBM for AI

sk hynix.key_metrics: HBM market share ~50%+ (dominant position). Key NVIDIA supplier for H100/H200 AI accelerators.
sk hynix.etf_impact: SK Hynix-tracking ETFs (7709.HK 2x long) are DIRECTLY impacted by: NVIDIA AI demand, HBM pricing/shipments, DRAM price trends, data center capex.

china a-share.overview: Mainland China stock markets (Shanghai/Shenzhen). A-shares denominated in RMB, primarily domestic investors with limited foreign access via QFII/Stock Connect.

key_indices.CSI 500: Mid-cap growth companies, more private sector exposure

china a-share.market_dynamics: Key drivers: PBOC policy rates, property sector health, consumer confidence, regulatory environment (tech, education, gaming), US-China relations.
china a-share.etf_impact: China A-share ETFs (2828.HK MSCI China A, 3010.HK SSE 50, 3033.HK CSI 500) are DIRECTLY impacted by: Chinese economic data (GDP, PMI, retail sales), PBOC rate decisions, property stimulus measures, regulatory announcements.

hong kong equity.overview: Hong Kong Stock Exchange - gateway between China and global capital. H-shares (Mainland companies listed in HK) dominate index.

key_indices.HSI: Hang Seng Index - top 80 HK-listed companies
key_indices.HSCEI: Hang Seng China Enterprises Index - H-shares
key_indices.HSTECH: Hang Seng Tech Index - tech giants

hong kong equity.market_dynamics: Influenced by: Chinese economic conditions, US-China relations, HKD/USD peg stability, global risk sentiment, Mainland capital flows via Stock Connect.
hong kong equity.etf_impact: HSI-tracking ETFs (83168.HK) are DIRECTLY impacted by: Chinese stimulus announcements, US rate decisions (HKD peg), tech regulation in China, property sector distress.

japan equity.overview: Tokyo Stock Exchange - third-largest equity market globally. Nikkei 225 is price-weighted index of 225 blue-chip companies.

key_indices.Nikkei 225: Price-weighted, similar to Dow Jones. Heavy tech/auto weighting.

japan equity.market_dynamics: Key drivers: Bank of Japan monetary policy, yen exchange rate (weaker yen helps exporters), corporate governance reforms (shareholder returns), wage growth.
japan equity.etf_impact: Japan ETFs (3115.HK Nikkei 225) are DIRECTLY impacted by: BoJ rate decisions, yen/USD moves, Japanese corporate earnings, Abe/Kishida policy legacy.

technology.overview: Technology sector spans semiconductors, software, hardware, internet services. High growth but rate-sensitive valuations.
technology.market_dynamics: Innovation cycles rapid. Key risks: regulatory scrutiny, interest rate sensitivity (growth valuations), China-US tech decoupling.
technology.etf_impact: Tech-heavy ETFs are sensitive to: Fed rate expectations, AI investment cycles, chip export controls, Big Tech earnings, cybersecurity incidents.

memory chip.overview: Memory chips (DRAM, NAND) are commodities with cyclical pricing. Current cycle (2024) seeing recovery driven by AI/HBM demand.

types.DRAM: Volatile memory for computing. HBM is premium segment for AI.
types.NAND: Non-volatile storage. SSDs, smartphones, data centers.

memory chip.price_drivers: Supply discipline (production cuts), demand recovery, AI infrastructure build-out, inventory levels.
memory chip.etf_impact: Memory-focused ETFs/products track Samsung and SK Hynix closely. Sensitive to: memory price announcements, HBM contract wins, data center capex.

leveraged etf.overview: Leveraged ETFs use derivatives to amplify daily returns (typically 2x or -2x). NOT suitable for long-term holding due to volatility decay.
leveraged etf.mechanics: Daily reset means compounding works against holders in volatile, sideways markets. Works in favor during strong trends.
leveraged etf.risk_factors: Volatility decay, tracking error, higher expense ratios, counterparty risk.
leveraged etf.etf_impact: Leveraged products (7709.HK, 7747.HK, 7347.HK) AMPLIFY underlying moves. A 5% underlying move = ~10% ETF move (before decay). EXTREMELY sensitive to daily volatility.

inverse etf.overview: Inverse ETFs profit from DECLINE in underlying asset. -2x means gains 2x when underlying falls 1%, loses 2x when underlying rises 1%.
inverse etf.use_cases: Short-term hedging, not long-term holdings. Severe decay in trending markets.
inverse etf.etf_impact: Inverse products (7347.HK Samsung -2x) are OPPOSITE of underlying. Samsung stock up = ETF down (amplified). Positive news for Samsung is NEGATIVE for inverse ETF.
